zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Matilde de Massey於1359年出生在英國藍卡爾利斯頓時，她的父親Sir Hugh Massey39歲，母親Alice de Wrenbury39歲。她於大約1376年與Sir John de Hyde在英國英格蘭藍卡爾丹頓結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於1460年在她的家鄉享年101歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Matilde de Massey于1359年出生在英国藍卡爾利斯頓时，她的父亲Sir Hugh Massey39岁，母亲Alice de Wrenbury39岁。她于大约1376年与Sir John de Hyde在英国英格兰藍卡爾丹頓结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。她去世于1460年在她的家乡享年101岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Massey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Massy, a pet form of Matthew . Compare Mace .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from one or more of several places in Normandy, such as Macey (Manche), Massy (Seine-Maritime), Macé-sur-Orne (Orne), and La Ferté Macé (Orne).
English: variant of Marsay (see Marcey ).

variants.name: Mace
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Masse, probably a shortened form of Matthew (compare Massey and Massett ) or possibly of Thomas .
French and Breton (Macé): from the French personal name Macé, a vernacular form of Mathieu ‘Matthew’. Compare Masse 5 and Maze 3.

French (Macé): habitational name from Macé or La Ferté-Macé in Orne, the former so named from Gallo-Roman Macciacum (from the personal name Maccius + the locative suffix -acum), the latter from the personal name Macé (see 2 above).

variants.name: Massie
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeen): variant of   Massey  .

variants.name: Marsee
meanings: English: altered form of   Massey  .

variants.name: Massee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Germain.
Possibly an altered form of English   Massey  .
French (Massée): from the feminine form of Massé (see Masse ). This surname is rare in France.

variants.name: Massa
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Antonio, Silvio, Annamarie, Biagio, Caesar, Carmela, Dante, Elisabetta, Ennio.

Italian: habitational name from any of various places called Massa, for example Massa Lubrense or Massa di Somma, both in Naples province, or Masse d'Albe in Aquila, all named from medieval Latin massa ‘farm, estate’ (from Latin massa ‘mass, lump, pile’, Greek maza). This surname is also found in southern France (see Massey 5).

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English personal name Ma(t)thew, a vernacular form of the Greek New Testament name Matthias, Matthaios, which is ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Matityahu ‘gift of God’. This was taken into Latin as Mat(t)hias and Matthaeus respectively, the former being used for the twelfth apostle (who replaced Judas Iscariot) and the latter for the author of the first Gospel. In many European languages this distinction is reflected in different name forms. The commonest vernacular forms of the personal name, including English Matthew, Old French Matheu, Spanish Mateo, Italian Matteo, Portuguese Mateus, Catalan and Occitan Mateu are generally derived from the form Matthaeus. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, including German Matthias , Greek Mathias and Mattheos, Assyrian/Chaldean Matti , Czech Matěj, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Matej . The name Matthew is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Mathai ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Mathew , see also Matthews .

variants.name: Macey
meanings: English: variant of   Massey  .
Americanized form of Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Macej or Mačej and Polish Maciej , derivatives of the Latin personal name Matthaeus (see Matthew ).

variants.name: Masey
meanings: English: variant of   Massey  .

variants.name: Macy
meanings: English: variant of   Massey  .

nameMeanings.name: Matilde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Germanic. See Matilda .

variants.name: Matilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Matilde de Massey was born in 1359, in Rixton, Lancashire, England, her father, Sir Hugh Massey, was 39 and her mother, Alice de Wrenbury, was 39. She married Sir John de Hyde about 1376, in Denton, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died in 1460, in her hometown, at the age of 101.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Matilde de Massey est née en 1359, à Rixton, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Hugh Massey, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Alice de Wrenbury, avait 39 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John de Hyde environ 1376, à Denton, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Elle est décédée en 1460, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 101 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Matilde de Massey 1359 in Rixton, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Hugh Massey 39 und ihre Mutter Alice de Wrenbury 39. Sie hat um 1376 in Denton, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom Sir John de Hyde geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Sie starb 1460 im Alter von 101 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Matilde de Massey è nata il nel 1359 nel Rixton, Lancashire, England, sua padre, Sir Hugh Massey, aveva 39 anni e sua madre, Alice de Wrenbury, aveva 39 anni. Lei sposato Sir John de Hyde circa 1376, nel Denton, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì nel 1460, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 101 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Matilde de Masseyが1359年にイギリスランカシュアイアリクストンで生まれた時，父Sir Hugh Masseyは39歳，母Alice de Wrenburyは39歳であった。約1376年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアデントンでSir John de Hydeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼女は1460年に故郷で101歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Matilde de Massey(이)가 1359년 영국 랭카실르 리스턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Hugh Massey(은)는 39세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice de Wrenbury(은)는 39세였다. 그녀는 대략 1376년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 덴턴에서 Sir John de Hyde(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1460년 그녀의 고향에서 101세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Matilde de Massey nasceu em 1359, em Rixton, Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Hugh Massey, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Alice de Wrenbury, tinha 39 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir John de Hyde aproximadamente 1376, em Denton, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ela faleceu em 1460, em sua cidade natal, com 101 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Matilde de Massey родилась в 1359 г. в местности рикстон, ланкашир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Hugh Massey было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice de Wrenbury, 39. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John de Hyde около 1376 г. в местности дентон, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Она умерла в 1460 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 101 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Matilde de Massey nació en 1359, en Rixton, Lancashire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Hugh Massey, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Alice de Wrenbury, tenía 39 años. Se casó con Sir John de Hyde aproximadamente 1376, en Denton, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió en 1460, en su pueblo, a la edad de 101 años.
